current_pass_description: Capsule policy control: determine whether substitutability claim in v15 Section 6.5 survives under last-epoch checkpoint policy, after discovering Phase 1 inadvertently used best-eval-loss policy due to a pre-refactor env var being ignored.
overarching_research_question: How few natural-language-labeled images are enough to fine-tune a modern VLM (Gemma 4 E4B-it via QLoRA) into a useful industrial defect detector, and WHICH KINDS of defects obey this 'tiger-analogy' (few-shot suffices because the model already has relevant priors) vs. WHICH KINDS don't?

passes.verdict_summary: All 14 N=30 cells keep. Mean AUROC lift +0.26 across 7 cats.
Label-source is nuanced, not universal winner. metadata wins on MVTec; agent wins on VisA candle; thin is within 0.01 of best on all cells.
NULL — rank=16/LR=1e-4/scale=0.5 did not improve over Pass 1 rank=8 recipe. Pass 1 recipe confirmed as well-chosen default.

NULL — stratified subtype sampling did not break metal_nut's degenerate F1=0.8136. 12 runs across 4 interventions all converge on the same degenerate solution. Metal_nut is a CONFIRMED honest failure mode.

passes.description: AUROC-vs-N curves on 3 cleanest categories, extended with tiny-N at the low end to test the tiger-analogy at its limit. Drop metal_nut (confirmed degenerate). Add N=1 and N=3 alongside the existing N values. If even N=1 or N=3 produces useful lift on distinctive categories, we have a headline figure showing 'one shot is enough for some defects.'
passes.hypothesis_primary: Curves on hazelnut + candle + DeepPCB show a knee between N=10-30. N=30 reaches >= 90% of full-train AUROC.
passes.hypothesis_tiger: Tiger-analogy test at the low end: if N=1 or N=3 already achieves AUROC >= 0.80 on distinctive-defect categories (hazelnut, candle, DeepPCB per defect_taxonomy.json), the 'one-shot tiger' story is supported — a strong standalone headline figure. If N=1 looks like zero-shot but N=10 already approaches N=30, the knee is in the single-digit-N region and that's the finding.

configs.notes: metadata labels universally (simpler paper story). N=1 and N=3 added per user's tiger-analogy hypothesis.

passes.verdict_summary: Knee at N=2 on hazelnut (97.8% of v0.1-cascade ceiling-lift) and candle (98.2%); DeepPCB shows delayed-knee shape (at-ceiling at ZS, lift only at N>=60). 81 rows, 3 seeds, recipe_version v0.3-curve.

passes.description: Fill remaining 12 MVTec + 9 VisA categories at N=30 with 3 seeds. Use the pre-registered defect_taxonomy.json to predict which will succeed (distinctive) vs which will reproduce metal_nut-style failure (subtle). If the taxonomy is predictive, it becomes a paper contribution as a practical rule-of-thumb.
passes.hypothesis_primary: Pre-registered distinctive-vs-subtle defect taxonomy predicts which categories show the N=2 fine-tuning knee (distinctive: yes; subtle: no/degenerate; mixed: scatter). Tested on 8 cats first (4D + 3S + 1M), expanded to 14 then 24 if the rule holds.
passes.hypothesis_taxonomy: The 'distinctive vs subtle' tagging in defect_taxonomy.json is PREDICTIVE — checked via ROC-style clustering of per-category AUROC against taxonomy tags. If positive, this is a standalone paper contribution: a practical rule-of-thumb for when fsvlm-style few-shot will succeed or fail, without running the experiment first.
passes.hypothesis_extractor_generalizability: Also re-run v0.1-extractor zero-shots on all 21 new categories to test whether the +0.15 to +0.34 AUROC recovery (our primary-candidate paper contribution) generalizes across MVTec's full suite, not just the 4 we sampled in Pass 2.
passes.unblocks_on: pass5b completed + user decision on paper framing

stages.description: Tier-A-minimum: 4 distinctive (mvtec cable, transistor, tile; visa fryum), 3 subtle (mvtec capsule, screw; visa macaroni1), 1 mixed (visa cashew). N in {2, 10, 30}, 3 seeds. Fastest defensible test of the taxonomy rule. If the rule holds (distinctive cluster at high N=2 lift; subtle cluster at low/null lift), proceed to stage2. If not, halt and pivot the framing.
stages.verdict_summary: Pre-registered taxonomy falsified; post-hoc lift~(1-ZS_AUROC) rule discovered.

stages.description: Tier-A-balanced extension: +3 distinctive (mvtec bottle, toothbrush; visa chewinggum), +2 subtle (mvtec wood; visa macaroni2), +1 mixed (visa capsules). Brings total to 14 categories. Run only if stage1 confirms the taxonomy rule.

pre_registered_prediction_for_stage2.rule: Few-shot lift correlates inversely with zero-shot AUROC.
quantitative_predictions: For each of the 6 stage2 cats, BEST_LIFT (max over N in {2,10,30}) shall obey: cats with measured ZS-AUROC <=0.55 will show BEST_LIFT >=+0.10; cats with ZS-AUROC >=0.70 will show BEST_LIFT <=+0.06.
Spearman rank correlation between ZS-AUROC and BEST_LIFT across the combined 14 cats (8 stage1 + 6 stage2) shall be rho<=-0.6 with p<0.05 (one-sided test).
N=2 mean AUROC shall capture >=80% of (max(N) - ZS) on at least 4 of the 6 stage2 cats.

pass_failure_definitions.rule_passes: All three predictions met. Rule confirmed on held-out data.
pass_failure_definitions.rule_partly_passes: 2 of 3 predictions met. Rule directionally confirmed but quantitatively imprecise.
pass_failure_definitions.rule_fails: <=1 of 3 predictions met. Rule not generalizable; report stage1 negative result alone.

pre_registered_prediction_for_stage2.no_post_hoc_revisions_clause: If stage2 fails or partly-passes, the predictions above are reported AS-IS in the final write-up. No goalpost moving. The honest reporting of the falsification PLUS this attempt at confirmation IS the finding.

stages.verdict_summary: Partly passes (2 of 3) per locked-in criteria. Spearman rho=-0.76 across 14 cats; macaroni2 missed quantitative band by 0.012 AUROC.

stages.description: Tier-A-full extension: +7 distinctive (mvtec carpet, grid; visa pcb1-4, pipe_fryum), +3 subtle (mvtec leather, pill, zipper). Brings total to 24 categories — the full Tier A coverage POSITIONING.md commits to. Run only if stage1 + stage2 results justify the extra compute.
stages.note: Held-out test #2. Run regardless of stage2 outcome — confirms or refutes the rule on a second held-out set.

pre_registered_prediction_for_stage3.explicit_drop: The stage2 per-cat ZS-band prediction (ZS<=0.55 -> lift>=+0.10; ZS>=0.70 -> lift<=+0.06) is NOT carried forward to stage3. It failed on visa/macaroni2 by 0.012 AUROC. Dropping it explicitly here, before stage3 runs, is structurally distinct from retroactively softening it post-stage3. The honest accounting: this prediction was too sharp; we are not attempting to recover it.
pre_registered_prediction_for_stage3.rule_summary: Few-shot lift correlates inversely with zero-shot AUROC; N=2 captures most lift wherever lift exists.
predictions: Spearman rank correlation between ZS-AUROC and BEST_LIFT across all 24 cats (8 stage1 + 6 stage2 + 10 stage3) shall be rho<=-0.6 with p<0.01 (one-sided).
N=2 mean AUROC shall capture >=80% of (max(N) - ZS) on at least 6 of the 10 stage3 cats.

pass_failure_definitions.rule_passes: Both predictions met. Rule confirmed on the cumulative held-out set of 16 cats (stage2 + stage3) outside stage1.

pre_registered_prediction_for_stage3.no_post_hoc_revisions_clause_v2: If stage3 fails or partly-passes, predictions reported AS-IS. The honest reporting of the stage1 falsification + stage2 partly-pass + stage3 outcome IS the finding regardless of which way it lands.

stages.verdict_summary: RULE PASSES (2 of 2). Spearman rho across 24 cats = -0.778, p<10^-5. N=2 knee on 9 of 10 stage3 cats.

passes.hard_kill_criterion: After stage1, if the pre-registered distinctive-vs-subtle taxonomy rule does NOT hold (distinctive cats failing to cluster at high N=2 lift, OR subtle cats failing to cluster at low/null lift), halt the staged expansion and pivot the framing to whichever category-feature actually predicts lift. Both outcomes are documentable; do not waste stage2 + stage3 compute on a hypothesis that did not pan out.

stage1_result.primary_hypothesis_outcome: FALSIFIED — pre-registered distinctive-vs-subtle taxonomy did not predict lift. Mean lift was distinctive=+0.031, subtle=+0.218, mixed=+0.314, opposite of registered ordering. n=8 cats, no overlap in ranges between distinctive and subtle.
stage1_result.post_hoc_rule_discovered: Lift inversely correlates with zero-shot AUROC. Cats with ZS<=0.55 gained +0.16 to +0.40; cats with ZS>=0.71 gained <=+0.06. N=2 captured >=85% of best lift wherever lift exists (5 of 8 cats).

stage2_result.held_out_prediction_outcome: PARTLY_PASSES (2 of 3 locked-in predictions met). Spearman rho on 14 cats = -0.758, p=0.00084 (PASS, well below threshold). N=2 knee on stage2 cats = 4 of 6 (PASS, exactly at threshold). Per-cat ZS-band prediction failed on visa/macaroni2 (FAIL by 0.012 AUROC).

stage3_result.held_out_prediction_outcome: PASSES (2 of 2 locked-in predictions met). Spearman rho across 24 cats = -0.778 with p = 0.000004 (PASS, well below threshold). N=2 knee on stage3 cats = 9 of 10 (PASS, exceeded threshold by 3).

final_outcome.summary: Pass4 (full Tier A) completed at 2026-04-28T09:09:59Z. Pre-registered distinctive-vs-subtle defect taxonomy was falsified at stage1. A post-hoc rule (lift correlates inversely with zero-shot AUROC; N=2 captures most of best lift) was discovered, formalized into quantitative locked-in predictions, and tested on held-out stage2 (partly passed) and held-out stage3 (passed). Cumulative result on 24 cats: Spearman rho = -0.778, p < 10^-5; N=2 knee shape holds on 18 of 24 cats. The original taxonomy claim is falsified honestly; the rule it was replaced with is held-out-tested twice.

passes.description: Baselines on the same splits: Anomalib PatchCore (first — SOTA classical), WinCLIP+ (if reproducible — most-cited few-shot VLM), PromptAD (if reproducible), raw HF+PEFT (no unsloth, no us). Stratify results by distinctive-vs-subtle taxonomy to test: does the 'tiger-analogy wins for distinctive, classical wins for subtle' pattern we observed in Pass 1-6 hold when compared against these baselines?
passes.hypothesis_primary: Anomalib PatchCore dominates on texture-periodic and subtle categories (carpet, grid, wood, metal_nut, pill, screw). fsvlm matches or approaches classical on distinctive categories and wins on VLM-unique axes (description, reasoning).
passes.hypothesis_extractor_generalizability: Run WinCLIP+ outputs through BOTH our v0 and v0.1 extractor style analyses. If WinCLIP+'s published numbers change under our extraction correction, the extractor-methodology finding generalizes to the frozen-CLIP literature too — a big methodology claim.

passes.description: In-context-learning (ICL) ablation — the ablation that answers a sharp methodological critique: 'your N=2 fine-tune result just shows contrastive-example matching works; you don't need fine-tuning at all.' We answer by running Gemma 4 itself in ICL mode (no weight updates, just prompt with reference examples) at matched N on the SAME test splits Pass 3 used, then comparing fine-tuned-Gemma-4 vs ICL-Gemma-4 head-to-head. If the fine-tuned advantage is < 0.02 AUROC, the reviewer is right and the project's contribution becomes the extractor-methodology + description-quality + taxonomy contributions (structurally inaccessible to in-context-learning).
passes.hypothesis_primary: Fine-tuned-Gemma-4 at N=2 does NOT meaningfully beat ICL-Gemma-4 at N=2 on distinctive-defect AUROC. If confirmed, the 'tiger-analogy via fine-tuning' story weakens and we lean on other contributions.
implementation_sketch: For each (category, N in {2, 4, 8}, seed in {42, 1337, 7}):
1. Sample N training examples (balanced good+defect) using same sampler as the sweep
2. Build a multi-image chat template: '[img_good1] [img_defect1] [img_good2] [img_defect2] ... Classify this: [test_img]. PASS or FAIL?'
3. Feed through base (unfinetuned) Gemma 4 — no adapter, no weight updates
4. Extract score via v0.1 logit-probability extractor
5. Compute AUROC on same held-out test split as Pass 3
Pairs with Pass 3 rows via identical (dataset, category, n, seed) for direct comparison. Delta = fine-tuned AUROC − ICL AUROC per cell.
cells_to_run: mvtec/hazelnut × N ∈ {2, 4, 8} × 3 seeds — our strongest fine-tuned result
deeppcb/pcb × N ∈ {2, 4, 8} × 3 seeds — where we'd claim 'first few-shot on DeepPCB'
notes: This is the methodological-honesty ablation. If fine-tuning beats ICL convincingly → tiger-analogy stays as a project claim. If not → the project's central contributions become (a) extractor methodology (+0.15 to +0.34 AUROC recovery), (b) description-quality eval (Pass 7), (c) pre-registered taxonomy predictive rule, (d) practical deployability (storage + inference cost of adapter vs retrieval DB).
ICL at N=8+ may exceed Gemma 4's context capacity with multi-image prompts. Cap at N=8 shots; if even that OOMs, reduce max_image_size and re-try.
WinCLIP+ / PromptAD in Pass 5 compare against FROZEN CLIP + retrieval. Pass 5b specifically isolates Gemma-4-family capability: fine-tuned vs ICL — no cross-model-family confound.
passes.verdict_summary: FT beats ICL at N=2 on all 3 categories (hazelnut +0.048, candle +0.098, pcb +0.163). Nuance: ICL at N=8 reaches 0.982 on hazelnut, above any FT result -- complementary across N spectrum, not "FT always wins". 27 rows, recipe_version v0.4-icl-baseline.

passes.description: Test a VLM-unique capability that classical methods (PatchCore, WinCLIP+) structurally cannot match. Take the best Pass 4 adapters per category; for each held-out test image, run the adapter with a 'describe what is wrong' prompt instead of PASS/FAIL scoring. Grade the generated descriptions against (a) dataset-metadata ground truth (subtype name + location if available) and (b) a small human-eval sample. Metrics: BLEU/ROUGE vs ground truth, defect-type accuracy, location-mention rate, and human quality rating on 30 samples.
passes.hypothesis_primary: fsvlm's fine-tuned adapter produces natural-language descriptions that accurately identify defect type and location on distinctive-defect categories. Classical methods produce a score, not a description, so this is an incomparable capability. If descriptions are accurate on >80% of distinctive-defect test images, this becomes the VLM-unique-capability contribution the project needs to differentiate from frozen-CLIP baselines.
passes.hypothesis_tiger_interaction: Description quality should correlate with the distinctive/subtle taxonomy from defect_taxonomy.json. Expect rich accurate descriptions on distinctive categories ('a large crack on the upper-right of the hazelnut shell'); poor or generic on subtle categories ('the metal nut looks normal but may have slight discoloration'). If description quality DROPS systematically on subtle categories, we have another axis validating the taxonomy.
implementation_sketch: Input: adapter path per category (from Pass 4), held-out test images, defect metadata from dataset
Prompt: 'Examine this {category}. If it has a defect, describe what is wrong and where. If normal, say so.'

Metrics: defect-class-mention (does text include the subtype word?), location-mention (top/bottom/edge/center mentioned?), BLEU against metadata-derived reference descriptions, human quality rating on 30-image sample (1-5 Likert)

passes.motivation: Discovered 2026-05-26 during 2D N x epochs sweep cross-check: Phase 1 (v0.4-longepoch-validation) used best-eval-loss checkpoint policy because the pre-refactor code silently ignored FSVLM_DISABLE_CHECKPOINTS=1. The 2D sweep (v0.5-2d-sweep-50ep) uses last-epoch policy (post-refactor respects the env var). On capsule x 50ep x N=10 x {seed 42, 1337, 7}, this yields 0.876 (Phase 1) vs 0.823 (2D sweep) = 0.053 AUROC gap on identical cells. The substitutability claim in v15 paper Section 6.5 compares 50ep x N=10 against 3ep x N=full; both sides of that paired comparison in Phase 1 were under best-eval-loss policy. This control determines whether the substitutability holds when both sides are under last-epoch policy instead.
passes.description: Run mvtec/capsule x 3 epochs x N=full x 5 seeds x Gemma 4 E4B-it under FSVLM_DISABLE_CHECKPOINTS=1 (last-epoch policy). Compare new mean against Phase 1's 3ep x N=full x 5s value of 0.891 (best-eval-loss policy).

H1_substitutability_survives.prediction: New 3ep x N=full x last-epoch mean lands at 0.84 +/- 0.02 (~ -0.05 from Phase 1's 0.891).
H1_substitutability_survives.interpretation: Both sides of the paired substitutability comparison shift down by ~0.05 under last-epoch policy. Substitutability holds at the lower absolute level. v15 Section 6.5 needs explicit policy labels but the claim survives.
H1_substitutability_survives.paper_action_if_confirmed: Path A: add '(best-eval-loss checkpoint policy)' labels to Phase 1 numbers in Section 6.5; mark 2D sweep figures as last-epoch; update Section 4.10b Pitfall #10 with per-cat magnitude note (capsule ~ +0.05).

H2_substitutability_weakens.prediction: New 3ep x N=full x last-epoch mean stays at 0.89 +/- 0.02.
H2_substitutability_weakens.interpretation: Only 50ep x N=10 drops under last-epoch policy; the substitutability claim on capsule fails (Delta becomes ~ -0.07, no longer within seed noise). v15 Section 6.5 substitutability claim weakens on this cat.
H2_substitutability_weakens.paper_action_if_confirmed: Path B: revise Section 6.5 substitutability claim from '6 of 7' to '5 of 7' with capsule moved to the failure side. Acknowledge the policy-confound in the substitutability claim's history.

H3_inconclusive.prediction: New 3ep x N=full x last-epoch mean lands between 0.86 and 0.88 (neither -0.05 nor unchanged).
H3_inconclusive.interpretation: Effect is partial; can't cleanly attribute. Report as a limitation in Section 8 and defer cleaner resolution to follow-on work.

passes.decision_rule_locked: If new mean is within 0.02 of 0.84 -> H1 confirmed, apply Path A. If within 0.02 of 0.89 -> H2 confirmed, apply Path B. Otherwise -> H3, report as Section 8 limitation.

global_notes: Every run carries git_hash + recipe_version + status.

Paper framing is deferred until after Pass 3/4/5/7 all land. Candidate frames: (A) 'N=30 is enough for tiger-like defects' with extractor as secondary; (B) 'Extractor choice dominates architecture' with few-shot as secondary; (C) 'A taxonomy-based rule for when VLM few-shot works' with both subsidiary findings as evidence.
The pre-registered defect_taxonomy.json is frozen at 2026-04-20T12:30. Any retroactive change to the tagging invalidates the statistical test and must be documented as a methodology failure.
Pass 7 is CRITICAL if we want to differentiate from frozen-CLIP baselines — it tests the VLM's unique capability (generating text) that classical methods fundamentally cannot do.
status_note: Pass4 (full Tier A) completed. See passes[].final_outcome for the rule-passes summary. Future passes (multi-model generalization, recipe stability, ICL extension at scale) are optional follow-ups.

multi_model_phase.outcome: rule transfers to 2 of 3 model families: Gemma rho=-0.778 (n=24, p<10^-5), Qwen3 rho=-1.000 (n=5, p<10^-4), Llama rho=+0.200 (n=5 lowest-ZS, p=0.63 — does not transfer under recipe rank=8/lr=2e-4/epochs=3)
multi_model_phase.qwen2_5_data_kept: v0.5-tier-a-qwen recipe contains 6 ZS rows from initial Qwen2.5 attempt (kept for cross-Qwen comparison)
